title: What Evolution & Animals Is
body:
Evolution by natural selection is the process that explains how living things change over time. Organisms vary, some of that variation is heritable, and individuals whose traits help them survive and reproduce tend to leave more descendants. Over long periods, those small advantages accumulate into adaptation, diversification, and the branching tree of life.

Animals are one of evolution's most remarkable outcomes. From insects and fish to elephants, birds, and octopuses, the animal kingdom shows just how many forms intelligence, movement, and survival can take. Some animals have complex social lives, some use tools, and some show behaviours that look surprisingly close to culture.

Human beings are part of this story, not separate from it. Our bodies, brains, and instincts all make more sense when viewed through evolution. Hunger, fear, social bonding, and pattern-seeking are not random features. They are inherited responses shaped by millions of years of selection.
keyPoints: Evolution explains how life changes over time
Natural selection is the main mechanism of adaptation
Animals show extraordinary diversity in form and behaviour
Human beings are one branch of the animal tree, not outside it
Animal cognition is broader than people often assume
furtherReading: 'On the Origin of Species' by Charles Darwin
